item.arguments: Use $fw:shape for a new tenant audit trail feature where the goal is clear but UX, scope, and success criteria are still open.
special_constraints: Behavior and scope are not clear enough for planning.
success_definition: Frames the output as requirements or a requirements plan.
Keeps the next handoff into plan and then work explicit.

item.arguments: Use $fw:shape to find the strongest next bets for improving this plugin before we choose one to build.
special_constraints: The work is not chosen yet.
success_definition: Frames the output as a ranked shortlist.
Does not jump directly into planning or implementation.
Keeps the selected idea flowing into brainstorm.

item.arguments: Use $fw:shape on docs/brainstorms/2026-04-18-audit-trail-requirements.md and get it ready for implementation.
success_definition: Frames the output as a reviewed technical plan.
Ends with a deepen vs work choice.

item.arguments: Use $fw:shape on docs/plans/2026-04-18-001-runtime-retry-plan.md and harden the rollout and test posture before we implement.
special_constraints: A plan exists but needs strengthening before work.
success_definition: Focuses on making the plan more execution-ready.
Does not re-plan from scratch without a reason.
Keeps work as the next main stage once the plan is strong enough.
